- **Sabre Split Screen** — designed and built a Java-based Sabre terminal emulator embedded in a web browser with HTML/JavaScript interface. Became the foundation of a product deployed to over 200,000 travel agent desktops worldwide.
- **Abacus Agent Online** — led joint project with Abacus to integrate the Sabre emulator into a web application for travel agents across Southeast Asia. Resulted in over $1M in incremental revenue.
- **Motorola Bluetooth** — joint project to develop and test Bluetooth devices and applications, including a wireless airline check-in system.
- **First mobile flight information** — developed the first mobile implementation of flight status lookup using a telnet gateway into the Sabre host system with an HDML interface.
- **Hotel Genie** — contributed to design and implementation of location-based hotel search integrating MapQuest with real-time room availability.

Overview
[COMPANY] (NDR) is seeking a Senior Platform Engineer, Data & AI to help build and scale the next generation of our enterprise data and AI platform. This role sits within the Data Engineering organization and will focus on building the application and service layer that powers our Customer Data Platform (CDP), AI products, and operational data systems.
A primary focus of this role will be designing and implementing cloud-native services in AWS that sit in front of core data platforms including PostgreSQL, Snowflake, and other enterprise data systems. These services will support internal applications, customer intelligence initiatives, marketing activation, analytics, and AI-driven products.
While this role will collaborate closely with our ML Ops team and contribute to shared platform initiatives, the primary emphasis is backend platform engineering, API development, and operational data services.
The ideal candidate combines strong software engineering fundamentals with experience building production-grade backend systems, cloud-native services, APIs, and data platforms.
Responsibilities
- Design, develop, and maintain scalable backend services and APIs that power NDR’s Customer Data Platform and AI ecosystem.
- Build secure, high-performance service layers on top of PostgreSQL, Snowflake, and other enterprise data platforms.
- Design and implement RESTful APIs and service-oriented architectures using Python, FastAPI, and modern backend engineering practices.
- Develop cloud-native applications and platform services within AWS using scalable, resilient architectural patterns.
- Partner with Data Engineering teams to expose governed data products and operational datasets through reusable platform services.
- Collaborate with ML Ops and Data Science teams to support model serving, feature delivery, inference workflows, and AI-powered applications.
- Design and implement authentication, authorization, auditing, and governance controls for data and platform services.
- Build and maintain CI/CD pipelines, Infrastructure-as-Code, testing frameworks, and deployment automation.
- Establish observability, monitoring, logging, and alerting standards across platform services and APIs.
- Optimize platform reliability, scalability, performance, and operational efficiency.
- Contribute to platform architecture decisions and help define engineering standards across Data Engineering and AI initiatives.
- Document system architecture, API contracts, operational processes, and engineering best practices.
Qualifications
Education/Experience:
- Bachelor’s degree in related field preferred.
- 5 years of experience building production backend systems, platform services, cloud-native applications, or data platforms.
- Strong experience building and operating APIs using FastAPI, Flask, Django, or similar frameworks.
- Strong PostgreSQL experience including schema design, query optimization, indexing, and performance tuning.
- Hands-on experience with AWS cloud services and modern cloud architecture patterns.
- Strong experience with Docker and containerized application deployment.
- Experience designing distributed systems and service-oriented architectures.
- Experience implementing CI/CD workflows and modern software engineering best practices.
- Experience with observability, monitoring, logging, and alerting frameworks.
- Experience with pytest testing frameworks and patterns, including unit, integration, and end-to-end testing.
- Experience with Bash and Unix-based environments.
- Preferred Experience:
- Experience deploying model-serving or inference APIs.
- Experience with ECS, EKS, Kubernetes, or serverless AWS architectures.
- Experience supporting Customer Data Platforms (CDPs), customer 360 initiatives, or marketing technology ecosystems.
- Experience with event-driven architectures, CDC pipelines, or streaming platforms.
- Experience in financial services, fintech, or other regulated industries.
- Strong software engineering fundamentals, including design patterns and maintainable architecture practices.
Required Skills/Abilities:
- Expert-level Python development experience.
- Strong SQL expertise and experience working with enterprise data platforms such as Snowflake.
- Familiarity with Infrastructure-as-Code tooling such as Terraform.
- Strong communication and collaboration skills across Data Engineering, ML Ops, Product, and Analytics teams.
- Ability to operate independently and help define platform standards and architecture direction.
[COMPANY] Role Qualifications:
- Computer competency and ability to work with a computer.
- Prioritize multiple tasks and projects simultaneously.
- Exceptional written and verbal communication skills.
- Punctuality expected, ready to report to work on a consistent basis.
- Attain and maintain high performance expectations on a monthly basis.
- Work in a fast-paced, high-volume setting.
- Use and navigate multiple computer systems with exceptional multi-tasking skills.
- Remain calm and professional during difficult discussions.
- Take constructive feedback.
- Available for full-time position.
Compensation Information
Our salary ranges are determined by role, level, and location. The range displayed on each job posting reflects the minimum and maximum target for each position across the US. Within the range, individual pay is determined by work location, job-related skills, experience, and relevant education or training. This good faith pay range is provided in compliance with NYC law and the laws of other jurisdictions that may require a salary range in job postings. The salary for this position is $140,000.00 to $160,000.00.
About [COMPANY]
[COMPANY] was founded in 2009 with the goal of helping an expanding number of consumers deal with overwhelming debt. We are one of the most-trusted and best-rated consumer debt relief providers in the United States. As a leading debt settlement organization, we have helped over 450,000 people settle over $10 billion of debt, while empowering them to lead a healthier financial lifestyle and feel free to live their best life. At [COMPANY], we treat our clients like real people. Our purpose is to elevate, empower, and transform their lives.
Rated A+ by the Better Business Bureau, our goal is to help individuals and families get out of debt with the least possible cost through conducting financial consultations, educating the consumer and recommending the appropriate solution. We become our clients' number one advocate to help them reestablish financial stability as quickly as possible.
Want to learn more about who we are? Connect with us on social!
Benefits
[COMPANY] is a team-oriented environment full of rewards and growth opportunities for our employees. We are dedicated to our employee's success and growth within the company, through our employee mentorship and leadership programs.
Our extensive benefits package includes:
- Generous Medical, Dental, and Vision Benefits
- 401(k) with Company Match

JD mentions "model serving" and "inference workflows" under responsibilities. So yes, model serving is mentioned. Also "inference" appears. So +1.5.

- ai_observability_evals: pattern `llm.?observab|ai.?observab|langfuse|braintrust|arize|openllmetry|llm.as.judge|eval(uation)?.?(harness|pipeline|suite|framework)|golden.?dataset|prompt.?regression`. JD does not mention observability specific to AI, just general observability. So no.

- ai_reliability_governance: pattern `ai.?reliability|llm.{0,30}(cost|token|budget|slo)|guardrail|prompt.?injection|model.?rout(ing|er)|hallucination`. JD mentions "model serving" but not specifically reliability or governance. No direct match.

- agentic_systems: pattern `agentic|ai.?agents?|model.?context.?protocol|\bmcp\b|tool.?calling|multi.?agent|agent.?orchestrat`. Not mentioned in JD.

- ai_platform_general: pattern `ml.?platform|mlops|llmops|ai.?platform|gpu.?(cluster|infra|fleet|scheduling|capacity)`. JD mentions "Data & AI platform", "AI platform", "ML Ops team", "AI-driven products". So yes, +1.0.

- austin_area: pattern `austin|leander|cedar park|round rock|georgetown|pflugerville|taylor|texas|tx`. JD location is US, but not specified. The JOB_META shows location empty. So no bonus.

- aws: pattern `aws|amazon web services`. JD mentions AWS multiple times. +1.0.

- terraform: pattern `terraform`. JD mentions "Infrastructure-as-Code tooling such as Terraform". +1.0.

- remote_us: pattern `remote`. JD has "#LI-REMOTE" and is remote-friendly. +1.0.

- comp_target: needs structured_comp. The posted salary range is $140k-$160k. The candidate's comp floor is $170k. So comp is below floor. This modifier is for meeting target, so not applicable.

- you_build_it: pattern `you build it.*you run it|build it.*run it|own.*production`. Not explicitly in JD.

- kubernetes: pattern `kubernetes|k8s`. JD mentions "ECS, EKS, Kubernetes" as preferred. So +0.5.

- cicd: pattern `ci/cd|cicd|continuous integration|pipeline`. JD mentions "CI/CD pipelines". +0.5.

- observability: pattern `prometheus|grafana|datadog|observability|elk|splunk`. JD mentions "observability, monitoring, logging, and alerting". +0.5.

- docker: pattern `docker`. JD mentions Docker. +0.5.

- platform_devex: pattern `platform|developer experience|devex|golden path`. JD mentions "Platform Engineering" and "platform services". +0.5.

- small_company: pattern `small team|startup|series [abc]`. Company is a debt relief company, likely not a startup (founded 2009, 450k+ clients). Not small.

Now negative modifiers:

- relocation_required: pattern `on.?site only|in.?office only|must.*relocat|relocation required`. Not present.

- hybrid_non_local: pattern `hybrid`. Not present (remote).

- city_only_no_remote: pattern for location_only. Not applicable.

- comp_below_floor: pattern `structured_comp` - the posted comp is $140k-$160k, candidate floor $170k, so below floor. -3.0.

- comp_marginal: not applicable since comp is far below floor.

- people_management: pattern `performance review|headcount|hiring decision|manage.*team of`. Not present.

- follow_the_sun: not present.

- large_enterprise: pattern `fortune\s*(?:500|100|50)|\b\d{2,3},000\+?\s*employees`. The company has 450,000 clients but not employees. Not clear.

- known_large_enterprise: pattern `cvs|walgreens|bank of america|wells fargo|jpmorgan|chase|citibank`. Not present.

- staffing_agency: not present.

- extreme_oncall: not present.

- oncall_low_comp: pattern `on.?call|on-call rotation`. JD does not mention on-call.

- k8s_5yr_primary: pattern `5\+.*years.*kubernetes`. Not present.

- first_line_support: not present.

- gcp_primary: pattern `\bgcp\b|\bgoogle cloud\b`. Not present.

- azure_primary_no_aws: pattern `\bazure\b`. Not present.

- compliance_heavy: pattern `pci.?dss|sox\b|hipaa|iso.?27001`. JD mentions "financial services, fintech, or other regulated industries" as preferred, but not required. So not a hard requirement.

- high_experience_bar: pattern `10\+.*years|15\+.*years`. JD requires 5 years experience, so no.

- mts_no_seniority: title not MTS.

- generic_swe_title: title is "Senior Platform Engineer", not generic.

- missing_location: pattern `no_location_no_remote`. JD meta shows location empty, but the job listing says "US" and remote. So not missing location.

- domain_mismatch: pattern `domain_mismatch`. The company is a debt relief company, not a tech company. The candidate's positioning is AI infrastructure. There is a domain mismatch because the JD is about data platform engineering for a debt settlement company, not AI infrastructure specifically. The JD mentions AI but mostly as a secondary focus. The primary focus is backend platform engineering for CDP, marketing activation, etc. The candidate's strong suit is AI reliability, not CDP or marketing tech. So domain mismatch may apply. Check: the JD says "Data & AI platform" but the primary focus is "building the application and service layer that powers our Customer Data Platform (CDP), AI products, and operational data systems." The candidate has no CDP or marketing experience. The AI part is more about model serving and inference, which aligns somewhat. But the domain is debt settlement, which is a regulated industry. The candidate's career is in travel, hospitality, finance, and now AI. Debt settlement is a new domain. However, it's not a complete mismatch. The negative modifier for domain_mismatch is -4.0, but we need to check if it truly applies. The candidate's resume shows experience in financial services (Fidelity, AvidXchange) which is financial, but not debt settlement. The JD is not specifically ML research or data platform in a way that would be a strong mismatch. I'd say it's not a domain mismatch per se, but the candidate might not be excited about debt relief. However, the rubric says "domain_mismatch" is a pattern, but we need to check if the JD matches a domain that is clearly outside the candidate's area. The candidate's primary domain is AI infrastructure, platform engineering, SRE. This role is senior platform engineer for data & AI, which is within the same broad area. So I'll skip domain_mismatch.

- crypto_web3_domain: not applicable.

- data_platform_domain: pattern `data_platform_domain`. The JD is about data platform. But the candidate does have data platform experience? Not explicitly. The candidate's experience is more SRE and platform engineering, but not specifically data platforms. However, the candidate has built telemetry platforms and pipelines. But the JD is about Customer Data Platform, which is a specific thing. I think this might apply. The pattern for data_platform_domain is a negative modifier of -4.0. But we need to be careful: the candidate's experience includes building an IDP for Mass General Brigham, which is a platform, but not a data platform. The candidate has no CDP experience. However, the JD is not exclusively data platform; it's also AI. So maybe not.

- ml_research_domain: pattern `ml_research_domain`. Not applicable.

Given the comp is below floor, that's a -3.0. Also the domain might be a slight mismatch, but I'll not apply domain_mismatch.

Now check hard blockers:

- onsite/hybrid outside commute tolerance: JD is remote (#LI-REMOTE). So no blocker.

- clearance/citizenship: not mentioned, but candidate is US citizen.

- never-claim tech as PRIMARY: JD mentions FastAPI, Python, PostgreSQL, Docker, AWS, Terraform, CI/CD, etc. All are within candidate's skills. No blocker.

So no hard blockers.

Now compute weighted score:

Base score: 4.0 (Senior Platform/Infra)

Positive modifiers:
- ai_inference_serving: +1.5
- ai_platform_general: +1.0
- aws: +1.0
- terraform: +1.0
- remote_us: +1.0
- kubernetes: +0.5
- cicd: +0.5
- observability: +0.5
- docker: +0.5
- platform_devex: +0.5

Total positive: 1.5+1.0+1.0+1.0+1.0+0.5+0.5+0.5+0.5+0.5 = 8.0

Negative modifiers:
- comp_below_floor: -3.0

Total negative: -3.0

Weighted score: 4.0 + 8.0 - 3.0 = 9.0

But wait, we also need to consider the base score might be adjusted. The raw score before modifiers is 4.0. Then apply modifiers. The final score is 9.0. However, the max score is 10. So 9.0 is high.

But we need to check the comp: posted salary range $140k-$160k. The candidate's floor is $170k. So comp is below floor. The modifier is -3.0. Also, we need to assess comp note: "Unposted comp is a flag, not a blocker." Here comp is posted, but it's below floor. So it's a blocker? Actually, the comp_check in the rubric: "comp_below_floor: -3.0" is a modifier. But the verdict rules say "SKIP: any hard blocker present, OR positioning is a clear mismatch, OR weighted_score below the RUBRIC skip threshold." The skip threshold is 6.0. The score is 9.0, so above skip. But comp is below floor. The instruction says: "Check COMP against the floor in PREFS. Unposted comp is a flag, not a blocker." So posted comp below floor is a strong negative modifier, but not a hard blocker. So we can still apply.

Now we need to decide verdict. Score is 9.0, no hard blockers, positioning: The candidate's positioning is AI infrastructure, SRE, platform engineering. The JD is Senior Platform Engineer, Data & AI. The primary focus is backend platform engineering for CDP and AI. The candidate's AI experience is more on the reliability side, but he has experience building platforms (telemetry, IDP, Seeker OS). The JD requires strong Python, FastAPI, PostgreSQL, AWS, Docker, CI/CD, observability. The candidate has all of these: Python (expert), FastAPI (Seeker OS uses FastAPI), PostgreSQL (experience from Hilton, AvidXchange? Not explicitly, but he has SQL experience), AWS, Docker, CI/CD (GitHub Actions, GitLab CI), observability (Dynatrace, Datadog, Grafana, etc.). The JD also requires "Expert-level Python development experience" and "Strong SQL expertise". The candidate has Python as a primary language, and SQL experience from working with databases. He has used PostgreSQL? Not explicitly in resume, but he has used SQL Server at AvidXchange? Actually, AvidXchange used SQL Server? Not sure. But he has worked with databases. The candidate also has experience with "Azure SQL Serverless" in telemetry project. So he has SQL skills.

The JD prefers "Experience deploying model-serving or inference APIs." The candidate has experience with LLM inference through provider routing and fallback, and Seeker OS uses multi-provider LLM routing. So that aligns.

The JD also prefers "Experience with ECS, EKS, Kubernetes, or serverless AWS architectures." The candidate has Kubernetes experience from Hilton (2016-2020) and is re-ramping. He has Docker experience. ECS? Not explicitly, but he has AWS familiarity.

The JD prefers "Experience supporting Customer Data Platforms (CDPs), customer 360 initiatives, or marketing technology ecosystems." The candidate has no CDP experience. This is a gap.

The JD prefers "Experience in financial services, fintech, or other regulated industries." The candidate has Fidelity (financial services) and AvidXchange (financial/payments). So that aligns.

The JD is for a Senior level, but the candidate is Principal level. The title might be a step down, but that's fine.

Now, comp is a major issue. The posted range is $140k-$160k, which is below the candidate's floor of $170k. The target is $200k. So the candidate would likely not accept this. The verdict should be conditional or skip? The score is high, but comp is a blocker. The instruction says "comp below floor" is a negative modifier, not a hard blocker. But it's a strong negative. The candidate's preference says "Comp floor: $170,000". So if the comp is below floor, it's a significant issue. The verdict rules: "SKIP: any hard blocker present, OR positioning is a clear mismatch, OR weighted_score below the RUBRIC skip threshold." Positioning is not a clear mismatch, but the comp is a clear mismatch. However, the comp is not a hard blocker per the rules. So we can still apply but with a caveat. I think the verdict should be CONDITIONAL because the comp is below floor, and the role is not exactly AI infrastructure but more data platform. The candidate might be able to negotiate, but the range is firm. So I'll go with CONDITIONAL.

But let's check the one_line: "Blunt one-sentence verdict." Something like "Strong platform engineering fit but compensation range ($140k-$160k) is below your floor ($170k) and the role tilts toward CDP/marketing tech rather than pure AI infrastructure."

Now, named_gaps:

1. Compensation: JD posts $140k-$160k, candidate floor $170k. Severity: high (since it's a dealbreaker).

2. Customer Data Platform (CDP) experience: JD prefers CDP experience, candidate has none. Severity: low (since it's preferred, not required).

Now, hard_blockers: none.

We can use the base score dimension and then the modifiers. But the weight is required. The rubric has weights? Actually, the rubric doesn't explicitly assign weights to dimensions; it's a point system. The output schema expects a weight for each dimension. - Comp below floor: weight 1.0, raw -3.0, weighted -3.0

But this is getting complicated. The output schema says "weight" is a float. I think we can simplify by having a single dimension "Overall" with weight 1.0 and raw score 9.0. But that seems too simple. The instruction says "Show the per-dimension breakdown." So we need to break down the rubric dimensions. The rubric has base scores and modifiers. I'll list the dimensions as the base score plus the positive modifiers and negative modifiers. I'll assign each a weight of 1.0 (since they are additive). The raw is the modifier value, and weighted is the same. The note explains the match.

Let's do that.

Now, bonuses_applied: list of positive modifiers that were applied. Penalties_applied: list of negative modifiers applied.

Comp: posted $140k-$160k, meets floor? No, it's below. Note: "Posted range $140k-$160k is below your floor of $170k. Salary negotiation may not bridge this gap."

Positioning: aligned? Not fully. The JD is more backend platform engineering for CDP and AI, while the candidate is AI infrastructure reliability. So note: "Role is primarily backend platform engineering for CDP and marketing activation, with secondary AI focus. Candidate's strength is AI reliability and infrastructure, not CDP. Some alignment on platform engineering and Python/AWS skills."

Company_fit: No company research. But the company is a debt relief company, established 2009, with 450k+ clients. Size bucket: probably mid-size or large. Stage: mature, not startup. Remote policy: remote. Note: "Debt relief company (NDR) — not a tech company. Established, regulated industry. Remote-friendly."

Tailoring: lead_with competencies that already exist in master. The JD asks for Python, FastAPI, PostgreSQL, AWS, Docker, CI/CD, Terraform, observability. The candidate has these. Lead with: "Python, FastAPI, AWS, Terraform, CI/CD, Docker, Observability" from the master. Also, AI inference and platform experience. Experience with model deployment and inference workflows." Do not claim: "CDP, marketing technology, Customer Data Platform, PostgreSQL schema design expert, financial services domain expertise."

Red_flags: Comp range significantly below floor; role is for debt relief company which may not align with candidate's AI infrastructure focus; JD emphasizes CDP and marketing tech which candidate has no experience in.

Senior Platform Engineer, Data & AI
Job Locations
US
ID
2026-6555
Category
Data & AI
Position Type
Regular Full-Time
Overview
[COMPANY] (NDR) is seeking a Senior Platform Engineer, Data & AI to help build and scale the next generation of our enterprise data and AI platform. This role sits within the Data Engineering organization and will focus on building the application and service layer that powers our Customer Data Platform (CDP), AI products, and operational data systems.
A primary focus of this role will be designing and implementing cloud-native services in AWS that sit in front of core data platforms including PostgreSQL, Snowflake, and other enterprise data systems. These services will support internal applications, customer intelligence initiatives, marketing activation, analytics, and AI-driven products.
While this role will collaborate closely with our ML Ops team and contribute to shared platform initiatives, the primary emphasis is backend platform engineering, API development, and operational data services.
The ideal candidate combines strong software engineering fundamentals with experience building production-grade backend systems, cloud-native services, APIs, and data platforms.
Responsibilities
- Design, develop, and maintain scalable backend services and APIs that power NDR’s Customer Data Platform and AI ecosystem.
- Build secure, high-performance service layers on top of PostgreSQL, Snowflake, and other enterprise data platforms.
- Design and implement RESTful APIs and service-oriented architectures using Python, FastAPI, and modern backend engineering practices.
- Develop cloud-native applications and platform services within AWS using scalable, resilient architectural patterns.
- Partner with Data Engineering teams to expose governed data products and operational datasets through reusable platform services.
- Collaborate with ML Ops and Data Science teams to support model serving, feature delivery, inference workflows, and AI-powered applications.
- Design and implement authentication, authorization, auditing, and governance controls for data and platform services.
